sutta: [Music] once while the exalted 1 was dwelling among the barghes on crocodile hill in the deer palm at bay sakala grove the good man the good house father nakula's father or nakula pita lasik and ailing grievously ill now his wife or nakula's mother spoke thus to him i pray good man die not fretfully ill is the fate of the fretful decried by the exalted 1 is the fate of the fretful maybe good man you think alas when i'm gone the good wife nakula nakula's mother will not be able to support the children nor keep the household together but think not so good man for i am skillful at spinning cotton at carding the matted wool and i can support the children keep the household after you have gone wherefore good men die not fretfully ill is the fate of the fretful decried by the exalted 1 is the fate of the fretful or maybe you think the good wife when i'm gone will go to another man but think not so good man for you and i know how for 16 years we have lived as household the holy life therefore good men die not fretfully or maybe you think the good wife after i am gone will have no desire to see the exalted 1 no desire to see the mounts of the sangha but think not so good man for verily when you are gone greater shall be my desire to see the exalted 1 greater shall be my desire to see the sangha wherefore good men die not fretfully or maybe you think the good wife after i am gone will not keep the virtues in full but think not so good man for so long as the exalted 1 shall have white robe women lay disciples folk with homes who keep the virtues in full i shall be 1 of them and if any shall doubt or deny let him go and ask the exalted 1 that exalted 1 arahan samasam buddha who dwells here among the bhagis on crocodile hill in the deer park at bay sacala grove wherefore good men die not fretfully or maybe you think the good wife will not gain the calm of heart within herself but thing not so good men for as long as the exalted 1 shall have white robe women lay disciples who gain that state i shall be 1 of them and if any shall doubt or deny let him go and ask the exalted 1 wherefore good man die not fretfully maybe good man you think the good wife nakula's mother will not in this dhamma vinaya win to the firm ground win a firm foothold win comfort dissolve doubt be freed of uncertainty win confidence become self-reliant live in the teacher's world but thing not so good man for so long as the exalted 1 shall have white robe women lay disciples folk with homes who in this dhamma vinaya win to the firm ground win a firm foothold win comfort dissolve doubt become freed of uncertainty win confidence become self-reliant and live in the teacher's world i shall be 1 of them and if any shall doubt or deny it let him go and ask the exalted 1 that exalted 1 arahan sama sambuddha who dwells here among the balgaes on crocodile hill in the dear palm and base akala grove wherefore good men die not fretfully ill is the fate of the fretful decried by the exalted 1 is the fate of the fretful now while the good man was being counseled with this counsel by nakula's mother even as he lay there his sickness subsided and nakula's father arose from that sickness and thus that sickness was laid aside by the good man of the house now not long after he had got up nakula's father leaning on a stick visited the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side and to him thus seated the exalted 1 said this it has been to your gain good man you have greatly gained good men in having had the good wife nakula's mother full of compassion and desiring your will or goodness as a counselor as a teacher verily so long as i have white robed women lay disciples folk with homes who keep the virtues in full the good wife nakula's mother will be 1 of them so long as i have lay disciples who gain the calm of heart within the self she will be 1 of them so long as i have lay disciples who in this dhamma vinaya win to the firm ground and live in the teacher's word the good wife will be 1 of them it has been to your gain good man you have greatly gained good men in having had the good wife nakula's mother full of compassion and desire for your will and as a counselor as a teacher that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so you see when this nakula's father came to see the buddha the buddha even before he said anything buddha because of their psychic ability to read his mind the buddha straight away told him and confirmed what his wife had said that she has the virtues in full that she has tranquility of mind that she has unshakable faith in the buddha this is this couple nakula's mother and nakula's father were a very loving couple and um they were like exemplary disciples of the buddha and um from this suta you can see here it is again to have a wise wife or a wise husband who encourages and supports 1 in the spiritual path was able to speak the right words at the right time the other thing you see here that when the nakula's mother was giving all this advice to nakula's father he recovered from his sickness which shows that much of our sickness is due to our frame of mind if we have the right frame of mind then we can recover from our sickness and also you notice the words of nakula's mother you can see how some lay people lay disciples during the buddha's time how they practice so well kept the precepts so purely practice meditation to calm the mind and have unshakable faith in the buddha dharma and sangha from listening to the buddha's words and live their lives according to the dhamma such people are rare to find even before and also now the nexuta
